resources.string: Tap to show only apps with unblocked trackers.
Tap to show only apps with granted dangerous permissions.
Tap this panel to focus apps with trackers.
Tap this panel to focus apps with granted dangerous permissions.
Tap an app for details. Long-press to select apps.
Revoke every granted dangerous permission for this app. You can re-grant individual permissions from Android Settings if needed.
Permission revocation requires root or ADB mode. Switch modes in Settings > Mode of operation.

plurals.name: installer_tracker_callout
plurals.item: ⚠ This APK contains %1$d known tracking SDK. You can block trackers from the App info screen after installing.
⚠ This APK contains %1$d known tracking SDKs. You can block trackers from the App info screen after installing.

resources.string:
Tap an app row to open its AppManagerNG details. Tap the app icon to launch the app itself when Android allows it.

Use the chip row to switch common views without opening the full filter sheet. The Sort chip opens the full list controls.

The status panel summarizes visible trackers and granted dangerous permissions. Tap it to jump into the most urgent filtered view.

Badges on each app highlight backups, trackers, and dangerous permissions. Tap a badge to go straight to the relevant detail tab.

Long-press a row to select apps for batch actions. Long-press an app icon for single-app quick actions.
Refresh the app list or review storage and profile access if apps should appear here.
Search terms, filters, or user selection are hiding every app in this view.
Active filters: %1$s. Tap below to clear them.
%1$s, %2$s, package %3$s, %4$s, %5$s. Double tap for app details.
Search is hiding every item in this tab.
Refresh details if this app should expose items here.
This section is unavailable with the current Android version or permissions.
This package does not declare activities in the current manifest.
This package does not declare background services.
This package does not declare broadcast receivers.
This package does not declare content providers.
No app operations have been reported for this app yet. Open the app and refresh to check again.
App operations require usage-access privileges that are not available right now.
App operations exist only for installed apps, not standalone APK files.
This app has not requested Android runtime permissions.
This app does not define custom permissions for other apps to request.
No hardware or software feature requirements are declared.
No OpenGL, keyboard, navigation, or touch configuration requirements are declared.
Package signatures could not be read for this source.
This app does not expose shared libraries or bundled APK/JAR references.
Current launcher does not support PinShortcut. Unable to create shortcut.

- AndroidFastScroll (Apache 2.0) 
- AndroidHiddenApiBypass (Apache 2.0) 
- APK-parser (BSD 2-Clause) 
- apksig for android (Apache 2.0) 
- ARSCLib (Apache 2.0) 
- Bouncy Castle (MIT) 
- Commons Compress (Apache 2.0) 
- Frost Icons (GPLv3.0) 
- HiddenApiRefinePlugin (MIT) 
- JADX (Apache 2.0) 
- LibADB (Apache 2.0/GPLv3.0) 
- libsu (Apache 2.0) 
- Material Components (Apache 2.0) 
- Material Design Icons (Apache 2.0) 
- smali/baksmali (BSD 3-Clause and Apache 2.0) 
- sora-editor (LGPL-2.1) 
- SPAKE2 for Java (GPLv3.0) 
- sun.security for Android (GPL-2.0 "Classpath Exception") 
- UnAPKM Android (Apache 2.0)

To the authors of 
- The Android Open Source Project 
- Absinthe's libraries 
- Activity Launcher 
- Apps_packages Info 
- AppsMonitor 
- AppOpsX 
- ChangeLog Library 
- ClassySharkAndroid 
- Editor 
- File Explorer 
- FlowLayout 
- Intent Intercept 
- Lingver 
- Magisk 
- Material Files 
- MatLog 
- Matrix Android SDK 
- OAndBackup 
- Open Keychain 
- SAI 
- Shizuku 
- tm4e 
- Watt
Allow AppManagerNG in Android settings to show screen time, launch counts, and network usage by app.
Usage access is off. Allow AppManagerNG in Android settings, then return here to load the report.
Try a feature name, permission, workflow, or preference keyword.
No usage in this time range
Try another time range or refresh after Android has collected usage stats.
%1$s, package %2$s, last used %3$s, %4$s, mobile data %5$s, Wi-Fi data %6$s, %7$d%% of visible screen time.
Do you want to uninstall this app?
This is a system app. Are you sure want to uninstall this app?
Could not enable the requested app op
Could not disable the requested app op
Saved component rules are inactive. New edits apply automatically; use the menu to apply older saved rules.
Lets you block any component of any app without explicitly turning on blocking for the app.
² prefix indicates that the trackers are in the ETIP stand-by list i.e., whether they are actual trackers are still being investigated.
Import/export rules, import external rules from Watt or Blocker.
No running processes are available yet. AppManagerNG will keep refreshing.
No processes match the current search or filters. Clear filters or search text to recover the full list.
System process killing enabled. Use with care.

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_clear_data
plurals.item: Could not clear data from %1$d app
Could not clear data from %1$d apps

plurals.name: clear_uninstalled_app_data_confirmation
plurals.item: Clear leftover data for %1$d uninstalled app? This cannot be undone from AppManagerNG.
Clear leftover data for %1$d uninstalled apps? This cannot be undone from AppManagerNG.

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_disable_background
plurals.item: Could not prevent %1$d app from running in the background
Could not prevent %1$d apps from running in the background

resources.string: Export blocking rules configured within App Manager to external storage.
Import previously exported blocking rules from App Manager.
Import blocking rules from Watt, each file containing rules for a single named package as packagename.xml etc.
Import blocking rules from Blocker, each file containing rules for a single package.
Version, changelog, licenses, contributors, and release trust details.

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_disable_trackers
plurals.item: Could not disable trackers from %1$d app
Could not disable trackers from %1$d apps

resources.string: Could not revoke all dangerous permissions
Could not revoke all dangerous app ops
These operations can affect many apps at once. AppManagerNG builds a review list before changes are applied; include system apps only when you understand the impact.
Find apps first, then apply tracker, component, or app-op changes from a reviewed list.
Create or restore app data after reviewing the generated package list.
Clean leftover data, trim caches, and optimize runtime artifacts with permission-aware safeguards.
External APK does not have any app operations
Add components disabled by others apps to App Manager. Be careful when using this tool as there can be many false positives. Choose only the apps you\'re certain about.
Find apps with tracker components, then choose which ones to block or unblock.
Search component signatures across apps before applying block or unblock changes.
Search app-ops constants such as 63 or RUN_IN_BACKGROUND, then apply the selected mode.
Review leftover data for apps removed with DONT_DELETE_DATA.
Clears the cache of all apps
AppManagerNG could not load the package list for this operation.
Input signatures with spaces, e.g., com.facebook org.app2 com.app3 etc.
Input app op names and/or constants with spaces, e.g., WAKE_LOCK 63 72 CAMERA etc.
Input app op names and/or constants with spaces, e.g., WAKE_LOCK 63 72 CAMERA etc. Use * to apply for all configured app ops.
Only works in root or ADB mode
Cache trimming needs root or ADB access because Android restricts cache cleanup across apps.
Changing app ops needs root, ADB, or system-level access.

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_unblock_trackers
plurals.item: Could not unblock trackers from %1$d app
Could not unblock trackers from %1$d apps

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_block_components
plurals.item: Could not block components for %1$d app
Could not block components for %1$d apps

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_unblock_components
plurals.item: Could not unblock components for %1$d app
Could not unblock components for %1$d apps

plurals.name: alert_failed_to_set_app_ops
plurals.item: Could not set app ops for %1$d app
Could not set app ops for %1$d apps

plurals.name: backup_exists_are_you_sure
plurals.item: Replace the existing base backup for %1$d app? Named backups are not changed.
Replace the existing base backups for %1$d apps? Named backups are not changed.

plurals.name: delete_selected_backups_confirmation
plurals.item: Delete %1$d selected backup? This cannot be undone.
Delete %1$d selected backups? This cannot be undone.

resources.string: Include system apps in this scan? Choose user apps only if unsure.
No tracker components were found in the scanned apps.
No apps matched the component signatures you entered.
No apps matched the selected app ops.
No leftover data from uninstalled apps was found.
Select at least one package before applying this operation.
Are you sure want to clear data from this app?
Could not find any such app
Permissions will be granted, app ops and components will return to their default values.
Installation was cancelled or the installer session closed unexpectedly.
Could not install this app because the package name is already in use.
This app is not compatible with this device.
The selected APK files are invalid or incomplete.
There is not enough storage space to install this app.
Installation failed. Review the APK and try again.
Your ROM is incompatible with Rootless Installer.
The operation finished with an issue. Check the logs before retrying.
Only the apps listed below are queued if you retry from this screen.
Retries %1$s for %2$s. Apps that already completed are not included.
No failed apps were reported. Review the log for the reason this result was shown.
Review the captured log before retrying or sharing diagnostics.
No logs were captured for this operation.
Review the APK details and installer options before continuing.
Could not access the APK files. Check storage permissions and try again.
Could not create an installer session.
Could not write the APK files to the installer session.
Could not commit the APK files for installation.
Could not close the installer session cleanly.
Backup name should not start with a digit nor should it contain any spaces. Leave it empty if you want to use current date-time.
Create a profile to save repeatable app actions and run them later.
This profile will be permanently deleted. Existing shortcuts that use it may stop working.
Runs the saved app actions in this profile. Choose the state to apply before continuing.
Runs this profile against apps matching its saved filters. Choose the state to apply before continuing.
Could not load this profile. It may have been moved or deleted.
This profile shortcut is no longer valid.
Adding a preset for backup options removes the burden of selecting flags every time you back up/restore.
Tap to allow App Manager to use OpenPGP
Could not request usage access, as the corresponding settings page does not exist.
Uninstall the existing app, then install this APK?
Could not install app because a system app with a different signature has this package name.
The existing data will be lost.
If you have turned off signature verification, you can use the Install only option to install the app without losing data.
This device did not report entries for the selected system configuration category.
Tap to view or send in signatures not yet present in App Manager\'s database of libraries.
Tap to submit the crash report.
Bundle device info, recent crash logs, and logcat into a ZIP for bug reports
Diagnostic report ready. Tap to share.
Spaces and unsafe filename characters are converted for the profile ID.
Allow the profile to be used in the routine operations
Clears the app cache of apps
Custom on/off state for this profile
Input permissions with spaces, e.g., com.permission.GET_ACCOUNTS android.permission.ACCESS_MEDIA_LOCATION etc. Use * to apply for all permissions.
Sign APK files before installing them.
Signature schemes, signing key, zip alignment, and APK verification controls.
At least v1 and v2 schemes should be selected for a greater compatibility. v4 scheme requires v2 or v3.
SourceStamp verified and identified to be from %1$s.
Enter the AppManagerNG KeyStore password. If you\'re seeing this for the first time, please use a password generator to generate a password and save it in a secure place before inserting it here.
Tap here to enter KeyStore password
Insert password for the alias %1$s. You can leave this empty if the password is the same as the KeyStore password.
Tap here to provide password for %1$s
No permission to display app ops
Apply tracker component changes to %1$s.\n\nBlock disables known ad, analytics, and tracking components. Unblock restores those tracker components if an app stops working correctly.
Choose the reset level for %1$s.\n\nClear cache removes temporary files. Clear data resets app state, accounts, databases, and settings and cannot be undone from AppManagerNG.
Apply background limits to %1$s by setting RUN_IN_BACKGROUND and RUN_ANY_IN_BACKGROUND to Ignore. Apps may stop syncing or sending notifications until you change this back.
Stop running processes for %1$s now. Apps can restart later from system events, notifications, or when you open them again.
This will uninstall or remove leftover data entries for %1$s. App data may be removed by Android and cannot be restored from AppManagerNG unless you have a backup.
Back up apps with Android KeyStore
Not all apps will work after being restored. Restoring KeyStore doesn\'t work on most devices.
No installed activity can handle this intent. Adjust the action, data, type, package, or user and try again.
Android, CPU, GPU, RAM, battery, and security-provider details.
Back up APK files from the source directory, including splits.
Back up OBB and media folders.
Back up cache, no_cache and no_backup folders.
Back up app permissions, battery saving and data usage options, MagiskHide status, SSAID, etc. Depending on the permissions, not all extras can be restored.
Back up rules configured within App Manager. Depending on the permissions, not all rules can be reapplied during restore.
Create a separate named backup instead of the base backup.
Restore backups that either fail checksum verification or have different APK signatures than their prior backups.
Sets Ignore mode for the following app ops: RUN_IN_BACKGROUND (from Android 7.0) and RUN_ANY_IN_BACKGROUND (from Android 9).
Backup location, encryption, import, restore options, and retention.
Select the volume or disk where the backups will be stored.
Could not find any volumes with write permission.
Perform backups only for the specified users
Review every installed app before starting a backup.
Create first backups for installed apps that have none.
Check existing backups and queue only those that fail integrity checks.
Update installed apps that already have a base backup.
Find installed apps with newer versions, data changes, or failed integrity checks.
Review every app with a base backup before restoring.
Restore apps that exist only in your backups.
Restore backed-up versions that are newer than the installed app.
Back up selected apps, data, and related metadata from a reviewed task list.
Restore selected apps and data after reviewing what will be reapplied.
The APKM file is DRM-free, no need to convert it to APKS.
Lock AppManagerNG using Android screen lock
Block disables known ad, analytics, and tracking components for this app. Unblock restores those components if the app stops working correctly.

Unblocked trackers: %1$d
Already blocked: %2$d

Changes apply only to this tab. Blocking known ad, analytics, and tracking components can reduce tracking, but may affect login, notifications, payments, or crash reporting.

This changes only the current tab. Blocking known ad, analytics, and tracking components can reduce tracking, but may affect login, notifications, payments, or crash reporting. You can undo from the confirmation message.
No tracker components in this tab

Use the switch to allow or block one component. Tracker chips mark known ad, analytics, or tracking SDKs.\n\nChanges apply immediately. Long-press a switch to choose the blocking method. Use the menu to review trackers, sort this tab, or apply older saved rules.

This tab is currently read-only because AppManagerNG cannot modify component states for this source or access mode.\n\nYou can still inspect component names, tracker chips, permissions, process names, and sort the list.
SSAID (Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID) is a device identifier assigned to each app (from Android 8 \"Oreo\" onwards). It is widely used by apps to track users.
You must restart the device immediately to use the new changes.
Enable or disable features in App Manager.
Please pick a screen lock in the Android settings, or clear app data.
Choose an installed app identity, enter a custom package name, or reset to the saved default.
Invalid key size for AES encryption. Key size can be either 128 or 256 bits.
Invalid key size for RSA encryption. Key size can be 1024, 2048 or 4096 bits.
No alias found in the KeyStore!
Install behavior, package origin, verification, and post-install optimization.
Import backups from OAndBackup, Swift Backup (3.0–3.2) or Titanium Backup.
Live logging is running. New entries will appear here as they arrive.
The current search or log level is hiding every entry. Clear filters to return to the full stream.
This saved log does not contain any readable entries.
The selected log file is unavailable or cannot be read from this location.
Display density, buffers, filters, and saved log behavior.
Restart log viewer window to see changes.
Log level at startup, when opening files, and when recording.
Show only the last %1$d logs in order to avoid out of memory errors.
Filter out the selected tags from logs.
Show full log text by default.
Please enter an integer between 1 and 1000.
When recording, write to SD card every %1$d lines.
Show process id and timestamp when expanded.
Please enter a valid number between %1$d and %2$d.

plurals.name: toast_log_truncated
plurals.item: Log too large, showing last %d line.
Log too large, showing last %d lines.

resources.string: Unable to save log. Did you enter a valid filename?
Omit sensitive info like web urls, phone numbers or emails.
Import/export Bouncy Castle KeyStore (BKS) internally used by AppManagerNG.
Are you sure want to replace the existing KeyStore? This action cannot be undone.
Component blocking, import/export, and rule reset controls.
Display changes in version, trackers, components, permissions, signatures, SDK, etc. in a version controlled manner.
Reject background data on metered networks
Allow background data on metered networks even when Data Saver is on
Allow background data when Data Saver is on
Port number is located under IP address & port section just below the Device name section.
Could not prevent %1$s from running in the background
Click on an item to allow or ignore it. Long click on an item to see other supported modes.
These permissions are defined by this app and cannot be revoked.
Click on an item to grant or revoke it. Only dangerous and development permissions may be granted or revoked.
Restrict App Manager to work with the selected users only.

Unlike the volumes, the selected directory will be used to store all the files related to App Manager, including saved APKs and backups. Storage Access Framework (SAF) is very slow, therefore, you should only use this option when others cannot be used.
Volumes with /tree prefix are folders selected using Storage Access Framework (SAF). Except these folders, the default directory for App Manager is a subfolder named AppManager.
The following is the AppManagerNG KeyStore password. Please store it in a secure place if you\'re going to back up/restore AppManagerNG KeyStore. This message will not be displayed again.
Remove temporary cache files across apps, including system apps.
Delete temporary cache files for all apps, including system apps? App data and backups are not removed.
Block tracking components after installing an app using App Manager.
Always install apps in the background and display a notification when finished.
The value must be between 0 to %1$d where 0 means maximum number of operations at the given time.

plurals.name: pref_thread_count_msg
plurals.item: Execute at most %1$d operation in parallel
Execute at most %1$d operations in parallel

resources.string: Click on an item to open the log viewer with the corresponding process ID as the default filter.
Select a directory containing the backup files (Swift Backup/Titanium Backup) or directories (OAndBackup)
Generic accessibility service to carry out certain operations such as clearing cache in no-root mode.
Method to use by default in places where there is no option to select a blocking method.\nNote: “IFW” does not work with providers, “disable” is used instead.
Block components using Intent Firewall as well as disable them. Recommended method for root users.
Block components using Intent Firewall only. Not recommended as some system apps can bypass firewalls.
Disable components only. Not recommended for root users since the apps can bypass this. In ADB modes, the test-only apps can be disabled with this method.
Activate the Internet features in App Manager
Depending on the Internet speed and load on the server, this may take a while.
VirusTotal and its logos are a trademark of Chronicle LLC. Neither App Manager—the API client—nor its authors are responsible for any data you may send to VirusTotal.
An API key enables App Manager to upload APK files to VirusTotal as well as fetch reports. Sign up at https://virustotal.com to get an API key for free.
Scan APK files with VirusTotal after you add an API key.

This app contains certain markers that indicate that it might be using Play App Signing. With this scheme, the signing keys are stored in Google\'s servers, and Google is responsible for signing the app. Note that matching signing information is the only way to verify that the app has not been modified by any person other than the developer (and in this case, Google too).
Could not change the app op mode.
Unable to use the current mode of operation. Falling back to no-root mode for this session.
Warning: Working on root instead of ADB mode.
Warning: Working on system instead of ADB mode.
Backup started. Keep the device awake and AppManager open until it finishes — Android 14+ may kill long-running backups in Doze.
Warn (full-access to the hidden API but issues warnings)
The file was modified. Discard all your changes and exit?
The format to be used for naming the APK files when you save them.
Trusted third-party apps can launch App Manager intents only when they include this key in the auth extra.
Regenerating this key immediately invalidates existing third-party integrations. Reconfigure trusted apps with the new key to keep them working.
The selected backup volume is not currently available. If it is located in an external storage, please insert it, or change the backup volume.
Tap to block ad, analytics and crash-reporting components in this app.
Components are blocked from running. Tap to restore.
Known tracking, advertising or analytics SDK. Safe to disable for most apps.
Component allowed: %1$s. Double tap to block.
Component blocked: %1$s. Double tap to allow.
AppManagerNG can use different access levels to manage apps. Pick the one that matches your device — you can change this later in Settings.
Detect the best available mode at startup. Falls back to no-root if nothing is granted.
Most powerful. Required for component blocking via IFW, full backup/restore, and direct privileged operations.
Not detected — only pick this if you know root is set up
Connect via Wireless debugging. Most features unlocked, no PC needed after pairing.
Run AppManagerNG on the same uid as ADB. Requires a PC the first time to start the daemon over USB.
Limited features (the system uninstall flow, reading info, launching apps). Works on every device with no setup.
Not active — enable in Developer options > Wireless debugging
Not enabled — turn on USB debugging in Developer options first
Tap a card to pick. Use the info buttons for details. Settings → Mode of operation lets you change this any time.
%1$s. %2$s Double tap to pick this mode.
%1$s. %2$s %3$s. Double tap to pick this mode.

A working root binary was not detected on this device. Picking Root anyway is fine if your root manager hides su until first request, but most operations will fail until you grant root.\n\nContinue with Root mode?

AppManagerNG checks your device on every cold start in this order:

1. Root (Magisk / KernelSU / SuperSU) — picked if a working su binary is present.
2. Wireless debugging (Android 11+) — used if you enabled and paired it.
3. ADB over TCP — used if a PC opened the daemon over USB previously.
4. No-root — final fallback. Limited features but always works.

Pick this if you are not sure. AppManagerNG re-checks on every launch, so if you set up Wireless ADB later it picks up automatically.

Root unlocks the most powerful operations:

• Component blocking via Intent Firewall (IFW) — survives reboots, can be applied to any app including system apps.
• Full backup/restore including app data, encrypted with your AppManagerNG keystore.
• Direct system-API calls without going through the limited PackageInstaller.
• AppOps and runtime-permission control beyond what Settings exposes.

Requires a Magisk, KernelSU, or SuperSU setup on the device. AppManagerNG will request su access the first time you trigger a privileged operation.

Wireless debugging (Android 11+) lets AppManagerNG run on the same uid as ADB without requiring a PC after the initial pairing:

• Most features of root mode are available — component blocking via PackageManager, AppOps tweaks, force-stop, clear data.
• IFW-based blocking is unavailable (root-only); the disable path is used instead.
• Backup/restore can read app APKs but not protected app data.

Enable Wireless debugging in Developer options > Wireless debugging, pair it once with your phone's pairing dialog, and AppManagerNG can connect from then on.

ADB over TCP runs AppManagerNG with shell-uid privileges using the ADB daemon already running on the device. The first connection requires a PC over USB to start the daemon; after that the device alone is enough.

Feature parity with Wireless debugging mode — most operations work, IFW blocking does not.

Use this on devices older than Android 11, or if Wireless debugging refuses to pair.

No-root mode uses only what Android exposes to a regular app:

• Launching apps and viewing their metadata.
• System uninstall flow (a confirmation dialog) for sideloaded apps.
• Reading the bundled tracker dataset to show which apps contain known SDKs.

Blocking trackers, force-stopping apps, clearing data, restoring backups, and most other privileged operations are not available.

Works on every device with no setup. Pick this if you do not have root and do not want to enable ADB.
%1$s — known tracking, advertising or analytics SDK. Safe to disable for most apps.
SSAID is a %1$d byte hexadecimal number i.e. a string of length %2$d containing 0 to 9 and a to f.
Display prompt before uploading a file.
The file was not found in the VirusTotal database. Do you want to upload it?
Net policy cannot be modified for Android core apps.
Open developer options page in Android settings
Use a completely black background when night mode is enabled.
There are no matching apps or backups for this operation.
The following apps cannot be restored because they do not have a base backup:
The following apps are not installed and cannot be backed up:
Delete the base backups for these apps? Named backups are kept.
Restore backups for the selected apps? Existing app data may be replaced.
Back up installed apps with the selected data types. Existing base backups are replaced only after confirmation.
Create named backups with the selected data types. Base backups remain untouched.
Restore base backups for selected apps. Existing app data may be replaced.
Choose one backup version to restore. Existing app data may be replaced.
Choose one backup version to install and restore this app.
Choose at least one data type.
No installed apps to back up
No installed apps are available for this backup task.
Installed apps with existing backups will appear here.
Every installed app has a backup
No first-time backup candidates were found.
All checked backups passed integrity verification.
No installed apps need a fresh backup right now.
Apps with base backups will appear here.
Every backed-up app is currently installed.
No backup version is newer than the installed app.
Delete the backups after importing them to App Manager? Each backup is individually deleted after it is imported with success. Select No if unsure.
Reload the list of apps stored in App Manager database in case of unexpected behaviour.
Re-open the privilege-mode picker and explainers shown on first launch. Your current mode stays active until you pick another card.
Show the main-list quick tour the next time you open the app list.
Quick tour will appear next time you open the app list.
Some scheduled operations (backups, observers, freeze rules) can be paused by Android\'s power-saving heuristics. Tap to manage AppManagerNG\'s exemption.
Exempt — power-saving will not pause AppManagerNG.
Optimized — system may pause AppManagerNG when idle. Tap to request an exemption.
Battery optimization controls are not available on this device.
Find out what\'s new in this release
A valid signing key is required for signing an APK file.
Freezes or unfreezes apps based on state
Method to be used by default in places where there is no option to select a freezing method.
This is the weakest freezing method. A suspended app may still appear in the launcher but will be grayed out and cannot be launched.
Recommended freezing method. It disables the app and along with all its components, but all the shortcuts will be lost.
This method is only recommended for day to day use. A hidden app appears as if it is uninstalled. But the app could reappear if it is reinstalled or updated.
Theme, typography, layout direction, and feature visibility.
Screen lock, authorization, Internet access, and session behavior.
Show plain-language guides, contextual help, and recovery hints. Advanced tools stay available.
Users, APK naming, concurrency, freezing, and expert defaults.
Language, theme, access mode, and app safety defaults.
Install, back up, sign, scan, and control apps.
Logs, files, rules, and advanced system behavior.
Device details, app info, and recovery tools.
Do not show launched app in Recents
Automatically freeze app when the phone is locked
Waiting for the phone to be locked…

To ensure the safety of your device and data, the version of App Manager you\'re using has been retired. You have to update it to the latest version to continue to use it, or uninstall it if no update is available.
This version of App Manager will expire very soon. Please update it to the latest version.

The app violates W^X policy and is capable of writing and executing in the same directory or in the same portion of memory. This allows the execution of arbitrary executables either by the modification of executables embedded within the app or by downloading them from the Internet. Unless this is the intended behaviour of the app (e.g. terminal emulators), it is recommended to find a newer version of the app that targets SDK 29 (Android 10) and later, or find alternatives.
Select format for exporting app list

It looks like USB debugging is not configured properly. Please read the documentation for additional steps. If you already know the steps, please click “Open” to open the developer options, or click “Close” to continue to use no-root mode.
Allow App Manager to display a floating window on top of all other windows
Allow App Manager to utilise the accessibility feature to track the contents of the leading window.
Queue DEX and layout optimization with reviewable settings before the operation starts.
This will queue runtime optimization for every installed package Android reports.

plurals.name: dexopt_scope_selected
plurals.item: This will queue runtime optimization for %1$d selected app.
This will queue runtime optimization for %1$d selected apps.

resources.string: Use Android\'s install default unless you specifically need another dex2oat profile.
Choose how aggressively AppManagerNG should rebuild runtime artifacts before the operation is queued.
Precompile layout resources on supported Android versions to reduce some first-render work.
Remove existing runtime profile data before compiling so the new profile starts clean.
Let Android use collected profile data when choosing what to optimize.
Ask Android to compile even when it would normally skip the package.
Run the system DEX optimization path immediately where root or system access allows it.
Requires root or system privileges in the active operating mode.
Choose a compiler filter before running optimization.
Choose a compiler filter from the list.
Aligning an APK file reduces its memory usage as the files in the APK can be accessed directly without copying the data to RAM. This step is required if extractNativeLibs is set to true.
The changes cannot be written into the file, possibly because it is located in a volume that App Manager has no permission to write to. Do you want to save it in a different place?
The application was uninstalled without clearing data and signature. Do you want to completely uninstall it?
Choose which source lists, safety levels, and app states are visible.
Refresh recommendations or adjust list options if installed system apps should appear here.
Current list, safety, or app-state filters are hiding every recommendation. Clearing filters shows every list entry, including unsafe and uninstalled items.
Search is hiding every recommendation in the current debloat list.
There are no files here yet. Use Add to create a file or folder, or refresh if the folder changed elsewhere.
App Manager could not open this location. Review storage access or try refreshing.
Perform DEX optimization immediately after installing the app without waiting for the system to do it when the system is idle.
This folder does not support creating a symbolic link.
An item named “%1$s” already exists in this location. Do you want to replace it?
Could not delete “%1$s” after copying it, possibly due to not enough permission.
Send notifications to the connected devices
Set whether to send notifications to connected devices such as wearables.
Choose where Android should install this APK and what installer identity or source metadata should be recorded.
Controls the installer package name Android records for this app.
These options can change package verification, update ownership, APK signing, optimization, and tracker blocking for this install.
Skips Android package verification for this install. Use only with APKs you trust.
Package verification remains enabled because this requires root, shell, or system privileges.
Re-sign APK files before installation when signature compatibility requires it.
Run DEX optimization immediately after install. This can improve first launch but may make installation take longer.
After installation, disable known tracker components when AppManagerNG has access for the selected user.
Unavailable for the selected user or package in the current operating mode.
Launcher visibility, home folder, and file manager session behavior.
Remember last opened path and its position
When this option is enabled, opening a new window opens the last opened folder instead of the home folder.
Run App Manager in the background
Running App Manager in the background reduces initialisation delay. Useful if you use App Manager frequently.
Lock App Manager when the device is locked.
The profile was modified. Discard all your changes and exit?

App Manager is trying to launch the activity via Search assistant (usually Google Assistant), but it\'s unable to do so due to insufficient permission. To open the activity, please activate Search assistant manually (which is usually done by long clicking on the Home button). Click on the Close button after you\'ve finished launching the activity to revert back to the original assistant.
VirusTotal requires the Internet to operate which isn\'t enabled. Please enable “Use the Internet” first.
Please navigate to the developer options to enable wireless debugging and generate a pairing code. Click Manual if you\'ve already opened the developer options.
If you are unable to use any of the modes, you can run the following command in any supported shell to run App Manager in privileged mode:
If you get a “permission denied” error with the above command, run the following command instead:
Use the system-default font instead of the material font. This is an experimental feature.
Apply the refreshed visual language: refined typography, calmer surfaces, tighter spacing and pill-shaped controls. Restart the app after toggling. Layouts are unchanged in this release.
Operations you run from profiles and batch actions will appear here for review or rerun.
Clear filters or search text to show saved operations.
Filter package, operation, mode, or target
This removes all saved operation history. It does not undo completed operations.
This removes the saved history entry. It does not undo the completed operation.
Run this operation again with the saved options.
Delete matching history entries? This cannot be undone. Completed operations are not reverted.
Automatically delete operation history older than %1$s.
Review the current capability state before running this saved operation again.
The current mode does not match the mode recorded for this operation. Results can differ if permissions or privileges changed.
This saved operation is marked as not replayable.
Legacy entry · saved before safety metadata
This entry was created before the operation journal recorded safety metadata.
No automatic rollback is available. Restore from backup or reinstall manually if needed.
Restore the previous backup snapshot if one exists.
Run the matching inverse operation from the same target set.
Uninstall the app or reinstall the previous APK if you saved one.
Review the changed settings and manually reapply the prior state if needed.
Delete the generated backup or export artifact if it is no longer wanted.

plurals.name: op_history_cleanup_visible_confirmation
plurals.item: Delete %1$d visible history entry? This cannot be undone. Completed operations are not reverted.
Delete %1$d visible history entries? This cannot be undone. Completed operations are not reverted.

resources.string: Add the first key-value entry to make this shared preferences file easier to inspect.
This shared preferences file will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
It force-stops and suspends the app to ensure that the app is not running in the background. This method should be preferred over the regular suspend method.
The app corresponding to the shortcut appears to have been frozen. Unfreeze temporarily and open the shortcut?
Set the installer app as the \"update owner\" during the initial installation to prevent other installers from installing the app without user interaction.
Record the origin or source of the app to be installed, reported by \"APK source\" in the App Info tab.
Ask the installer to skip running the package verifier (e.g., Play Protect) during the installation.
Back up data in ADB format. This option supersedes \"Internal data\" and \"External data\" options.
Usage at %1$s is %2$.0f minutes. Bar %3$d of %4$d
Usage on %1$s is %2$.lf hours. Bar %3$d of %4$d
Usage on %1$s is %2$.0f minutes. Bar %3$d of %4$d

The system is currently using %s memory out of %s, and the amount of available memory is %s. Among the occupied memory, the applications are using %s, %s are cached, and %s are used as memory buffers.
Memory usage is unavailable right now.
The system has allocated %s for swap and currently using %s.
Review dangerous permission groups, inspect app-level grants, and use guarded bulk actions when the scope is clear.

This view lists apps that request the selected permission group.

• Use an app switch to grant or revoke that app only.
• Use bulk actions after reviewing the group summary and confirmation dialog.
• Bulk revoke skips known OS- and vendor-critical packages to reduce reboot and service-breakage risk.

Individual app switches remain available for power-user recovery or testing. Treat critical system packages carefully.

Re-grants dangerous permissions to known OS- and vendor-critical packages, then clears AppManager permission rules saved for those packages.\n\nUse this if a bulk revoke disrupted phone, location, messaging, or other system services.
Restored permissions on %1$d packages (%2$d permissions re-granted, %3$d stale rules cleared).
Could not restore system app permissions.
No installed apps request %1$s. Try another permission group or refresh after installing apps.
Revokes %1$s from every editable app in this list. Known critical system packages are skipped during bulk revoke.
Grants %1$s to every editable app in this list. Apps may gain access immediately after the change.
Apps that can take photos and record video.
Apps that can access precise, coarse, or background location.
Apps that can read or modify your contacts and accounts.
Apps that can send, receive, or read SMS, MMS and cell-broadcast messages.
Apps that can place calls, read phone state, or access call logs.
Apps that can read or write files, photos, video and audio.
Apps that can read or modify your calendar events.
Apps that can access heart rate and other body sensors.
Apps that can detect steps and physical activity.
Apps that can scan, connect to, or advertise to Bluetooth and Wi-Fi devices.
